units.content: Huskova et al. compare AlphaFold2, ESMFold, and OmegaFold for speed vs. precision in protein folding, in Frontiers in Genetics 16:1715037, January 2026
This study represents the nearest approach to a structure-prediction failure predictor in the observational literature
The proximity to a failure-prediction framing elevates the FTO concern relative to other observational comparisons
Specification §4.14 characterizes Huskova et al. as using protein-language-model embeddings and pLDDT confidence scores as predictor inputs
Huskova et al. uses per-sequence confidence signals (pLDDT, PLM embeddings) as inputs, not architectural-attribute set differences of the comparator instruments themselves
The input space of Huskova et al. is causally distinct from claim 8a's input space, which operates on architectural attributes of the predictors rather than their output confidence signals

Despite being the nearest observational approach, Huskova et al. is distinguished from claim 8a by its reliance on per-sequence output signals rather than architectural-attribute set arithmetic, mitigating FTO risk
